description_long: This hammer features a compact, rectangular head with a metallic finish, accented by two horizontal bands and a central vertical stripe, suggesting reinforced construction. The handle is straight and appears to be made of wood, with a warm brown tone and a slightly tapered end for grip. The head is robust and blocky, lacking any visible claw or peen, indicating it is likely a sledge or mallet type, suitable for heavy-duty striking tasks. The handle is attached centrally to the head, and the proportions suggest it is designed for one-handed use. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. The hammer appears clean and well-maintained, with no signs of wear or damage.
description: A compact, blocky-headed hammer with a straight wooden handle and metallic accents, designed for heavy-duty striking. The hammer is clean, utilitarian, and shows no visible wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular metallic head with two horizontal bands and the straight wooden handle centered below.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness of the head and the alignment of the handle, showing the hammer's depth.
description_view_2: Top-down view reveals the square face of the hammer head and the full width of the handle attachment.
description_view_3: Rear angled view displays the back of the head and the handle's end, emphasizing the hammer's symmetry.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a slightly tapered end, smooth finish, no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Blocky, rectangular sledge-type head with two horizontal metallic bands and a central vertical stripe, no claw or peen.
